---
title: 'catalog add-alias'
---

Attach an alias to an existing catalog entry. Aliases are stable, human-readable
names you can use anywhere an entry name is accepted.

## Usage

```bash
xorq catalog add-alias <name> <alias> [OPTIONS]
```

## Arguments

- **`name`** — The existing entry's name (use [`catalog list`](list.qmd) to see options).
- **`alias`** — The alias to register.

## Options

| Option | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--sync` / `--no-sync` | `--sync` | Push the catalog to its remote(s) after adding the alias. |

## Examples

```bash
xorq catalog add-alias f02d28198715 penguins-prod
```

To add an alias at the same time you add an entry, use `xorq catalog add -a`.

## See also

- [`catalog list-aliases`](list-aliases.qmd) — list registered aliases
- [`catalog remove-alias`](remove-alias.qmd) — remove an alias
